feature: MillenniumIT Gap Closure Sprint 1 — Must Fix Before Go-Live
requirement: Address 5 critical gaps: market-wide halt cascade, full surveillance patterns, CSD↔corporate actions, settlement accrued interest, throttle admin API

tasks.title: Market-wide halt cascade + segment halt
tasks.description:
Halting a market or segment should cascade to halt all instruments in that scope.

Modify src/securities-service/internal/server/handlers_market.go:
- PUT /api/v1/securities/markets/{id}/status with status=HALTED: iterate all segments in this market, for each segment iterate all instruments with that segment_id, update each instrument's trading_status to HALTED. Log audit entry for each.
- Add similar cascade for segments: PUT /api/v1/securities/segments/{id}/status with HALTED cascades to all instruments in that segment.
- When market/segment is set to ACTIVE: cascade ACTIVE to all instruments (unless individually halted).

Also add to handlers_instrument.go: when creating/updating instrument, if the instrument's segment or market is HALTED, reject the operation.

Update server.go if new routes needed. Add gateway routes.

Go at /usr/local/go/bin/go. cd src/securities-service && go build ./... && go test ./... && cd ../gateway && go build ./...

tasks.title: 8 additional surveillance patterns
tasks.description:
Add 8 new surveillance alert patterns to the matching engine and surveillance store.

Modify src/securities-service/internal/engine/engine.go checkSurveillance() method:
Currently checks LARGE_TRADE and WASH_TRADE. Add:
1. FRONT_RUNNING — detect if a participant trades ahead of a large client order (check if participant has a resting order, then a large order from different participant fills, then original participant's order fills at moved price)
2. SPOOFING — detect submit then cancel pattern (track order lifetime; if cancelled within threshold seconds with no fill, flag)
3. LAYERING — detect multiple orders on one side creating artificial depth (>N orders from same participant on same side within threshold time)
4. PRICE_MANIPULATION_UP — detect series of buy orders pushing price up >X% from reference
5. PRICE_MANIPULATION_DOWN — same for sells
6. INSIDER_TRADING — detect unusual volume spike before corporate action announcement (check if trade volume > N * average and a corporate action is pending)
7. CUMULATED_VOLUME — detect when cumulative daily volume for a participant exceeds threshold
8. HIGH_EXECUTION — detect when execution ratio (filled/submitted) exceeds threshold for a participant

For MVP: implement checks 2-5 + 7-8 in the matching engine (they can be checked per-trade). Checks 1 and 6 require historical cross-reference and can be simplified:
- FRONT_RUNNING: flag if same participant has orders on both sides and one fills within 1 second of a large opposite-side fill
- INSIDER_TRADING: flag if trade happens on an instrument that has a PENDING corporate action

Add alert type constants to types.go. Update threshold store to support new types.

Go at /usr/local/go/bin/go. cd src/securities-service && go build ./... && go test ./...

tasks.title: CSD ↔ corporate actions wiring + settlement accrued interest
tasks.description:
Wire corporate actions through CSD custody accounts and add accrued interest to bond settlement.

Part A — CSD ↔ corporate actions:
Modify src/securities-service/internal/server/handlers_corporate_actions.go processEntitlements:
- After creating entitlements, for each entitled participant:
  1. Find their CSD custody account (via custodyAccountStore)
  2. If DIVIDEND: create a credit entry on the custody balance (increase available qty by entitlement value / par value, or just record cash entitlement)
  3. If STOCK_SPLIT: adjust custody balance quantity by split ratio
  4. Log audit entry for each CSD balance update
- This requires the corporate action handler to access custodyAccountStore and custodyBalanceStore

Part B — Settlement accrued interest:
Modify src/securities-service/internal/settlement/engine.go ProcessSettlementCycle:
- When processing settlement for a bond instrument:
  1. Check if instrument is a bond (look up in bondStore)
  2. If bond: calculate accrued interest using the bond's day_count_convention
  3. Add accrued interest to the settlement obligation's net_amount (dirty price = clean price + accrued interest)
  4. Store accrued interest amount on the settlement obligation
- Add AccruedInterest float64 field to SettlementObligation type

Go at /usr/local/go/bin/go. cd src/securities-service && go build ./... && go test ./...

tasks.title: Throttle admin API — per-firm configurable rate limits
tasks.description:
Replace hardcoded 100/sec throttle with per-firm configurable limits.

Add to types.go:
- ThrottleConfig struct: FirmID, MaxOrdersPerSecond int, Enabled bool, UpdatedAt — json tags

Add to store.go:
- ThrottleConfigStore interface: Get(firmID), Set(*ThrottleConfig), List, Delete + InMemory

Create src/securities-service/internal/server/handlers_throttle.go:
- GET /api/v1/securities/throttle-config — list all configs
- GET /api/v1/securities/throttle-config/{firm_id} — get config for firm
- PUT /api/v1/securities/throttle-config/{firm_id} — set config {max_orders_per_second, enabled}
- DELETE /api/v1/securities/throttle-config/{firm_id} — remove (falls back to default)

Modify handlers_order.go handleSubmitOrder throttle check:
- Instead of hardcoded 100, look up ThrottleConfig for the participant's firm
- If config exists and enabled: use config.MaxOrdersPerSecond
- If no config: use default 100/sec
- Return 429 with the actual limit in the error message

Update server.go + main.go + gateway routes.

Go at /usr/local/go/bin/go. cd src/securities-service && go build ./... && go test ./... && cd ../gateway && go build ./...

tasks.title: Unit tests for all Sprint 1 features
tasks.description:
Write tests for market halt cascade, surveillance patterns, CSD wiring, settlement accrued interest, throttle config.

Tests:

1. Market halt cascade:
- TestHaltMarket_CascadesToInstruments — halt market → all instruments in that market become HALTED
- TestHaltSegment_CascadesToInstruments — halt segment → instruments in that segment HALTED
- TestResumeMarket_CascadesToInstruments — resume → instruments ACTIVE

2. Surveillance patterns:
- TestSurveillance_Spoofing — submit then cancel within threshold → alert
- TestSurveillance_Layering — N orders same side same participant → alert
- TestSurveillance_PriceManipulation — price moved >X% → alert
- TestSurveillance_CumulatedVolume — daily volume exceeds threshold → alert
- TestSurveillance_InsiderTrading — trade on instrument with pending CA → alert

3. CSD ↔ corporate actions:
- TestDividend_UpdatesCSDBalance — process dividend → custody balance updated
- TestStockSplit_UpdatesCSDBalance — split → quantities adjusted

4. Settlement accrued interest:
- TestSettlement_BondAccruedInterest — bond settlement includes accrued interest in net_amount

5. Throttle config:
- TestThrottleConfig_CRUD
- TestThrottle_UsesPerFirmConfig — set 50/sec for firm, verify 51st order rejected
- TestThrottle_DefaultWhenNoConfig — no config → uses 100/sec default

Go at /usr/local/go/bin/go. cd src/securities-service && go test ./... -race -cover
